data.title: FIFA Presidential Association announces "FIFA Coin" plan
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: FIFA President Gianni Infantino announced the "FIFA Coin" plan at the White House Crypto Summit, hoping to launch a cryptocurrency that aims to take advantage of the $17 billion football industry and 5 billion fans worldwide, and will invite cryptocurrency experts to work together to help realize the project. (Bitcoin.com)

data.title: Coinbase CEO: Plans to hire 1,000 employees in the United States this year
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong said on the X platform that the White House Digital Asset Summit was a historic day, and the United States now has strategic Bitcoin reserves and emerging regulatory clarity. This directly translates into economic growth in the United States. Due to this new growth, Coinbase plans to recruit about 1,000 employees in the United States this year and will continue to build this industry in the United States to ensure that the United States maintains its leading position in technology and finance.

data.title: Crypto exchange Gemini has submitted IPO applications to Goldman Sachs and Citigroup
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Cointelegraph published an article on the X platform stating that Gemini, a cryptocurrency exchange supported by the Winklevoss twin brothers, has secretly submitted an initial public offering (IPO) application to Goldman Sachs and Citigroup.

data.title: OCC Clarifies Regulations For Banks Engaging In Cryptocurrency Activities
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on March 8, the U.S. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) issued a new interpretive letter allowing federally regulated banks to engage in cryptocurrency custody, stablecoin-related activities, and blockchain node operations without prior approval. The OCC also withdrew its 2023 statement concerning banks' crypto liquidity risks.

Acting Comptroller Rodney E. Hood stated that the OCC expects banks to maintain robust risk management controls for new banking activities, similar to those for traditional operations. He emphasized that the recent measures aim to reduce the burden on banks engaging in crypto-related activities and ensure consistent treatment under OCC supervision, regardless of the underlying technology. Hood affirmed his commitment to effective yet non-excessive regulation while maintaining a strong federal banking system.

data.title: Top US banking regulator rescinds previous digital asset restrictions amid Trump’s push to end crypto debanking
data.description:
The U.S. OCC set out to clarify that crypto activities are allowed in the federal banking system, rescinding its previous stance.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/345324/top-us-banking-regulator-rescinds-previous-digital-asset-restrictions-amid-trumps-push-to-end-crypto-debanking?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Dormant Bitcoin Wallet Linked To Dark Web Market Transfers Millions
data.description: According to BlockBeats, a Bitcoin wallet associated with the dark web market Nucleus has become active, transferring $77.5 million worth of Bitcoin to three new addresses. The main wallet still holds $365 million in Bitcoin. This wallet had been inactive since Nucleus shut down in April 2016.
